metadata.note: Demo backlog. It is intentionally self-referential: the backlog items describe the platform's own governance components, so `npm run setup` produces a database you can immediately query and a worked example of the SQL-first model.

epics.name: Governance Core
epics.summary: Risk-class guardrails, append-only audit, AI-security evals, and delivery metrics.

epics.name: Context Discipline
epics.summary: SQL-first backlog and the context broker that selects bounded, cheap context packets.

features.title: Risk-class guardrail policy
features.architecture: Declarative deny-by-default policy in config/agentic/guardrails.json, validated by a standalone checker. Risk classes map tools to confirmation requirements and required evidence.
tasks: Define risk classes and default-deny decision
Map tools to risk classes, evidence, and confirmation requirements
Validate the policy shape in scripts/guardrail-check.mjs
testCases: Policy with defaultDecision other than deny fails validation
A tool whose risk class requires confirmation but is missing requiresConfirmation fails validation
successCriteria: guardrails:check exits non-zero on an invalid policy
destructive tools are denied by default
useCases: An agent checks whether an action it is about to take is allowed and whether it needs confirmation

personaWorkflows.workflow: Check a tool decision before acting

features.title: Append-only audit log
features.architecture: Event-sourced JSONL log mirrored into the audit_events table. Never rewritten; corrections are appended as comment/decision events.
tasks: Append events to data/audit/audit-log.jsonl with stable ids
Validate parseability, required fields, unique ids, and basic secret redaction
Mirror the log into the audit_events table on setup
testCases: A duplicate event id fails audit:validate
A summary containing an obvious secret token is flagged
successCriteria: audit:validate exits non-zero on a malformed log
audit events survive a database rebuild because they live in the JSONL
useCases: A future reviewer reconstructs why a gate was waived from the audit decision event

personaWorkflows.workflow: Record an audit event before finishing material work

features.title: Agent evals and AI-security scenarios
features.architecture: Scenario fixtures mapped to OWASP LLM and NIST AI-RMF risks, scored against the guardrail policy. Results mirrored into SQLite/SQL/JSON.
tasks: Author deny scenarios for prompt injection and destructive commands
Author allow scenarios for read-only and degraded-safe paths
Score scenarios against the guardrail policy and fail on mismatch
testCases: prompt-injection-deny resolves to a blocked decision
destructive-command-deny requires confirmation and is denied
successCriteria: agent:evals exits non-zero if any scenario expectation is violated
useCases: A change to the guardrail policy is regression-tested against known adversarial inputs

personaWorkflows.workflow: Run AI-security scenarios in the local gate

features.title: DORA-style delivery metrics
features.architecture: Local proxies for deployment frequency, change lead time, and change failure rate derived from git history and the audit log.
tasks: Derive deployment-frequency and lead-time proxies from git and the audit log
Derive a change-failure-rate proxy from failure/rollback signals
Mirror metrics into SQLite/SQL/JSON
testCases: metrics:dora produces a JSON payload even with an empty audit log
successCriteria: metrics:dora runs offline against local git and the audit log
useCases: A solo maintainer tracks delivery health without an external DevOps platform

features.title: SQL-first backlog engine
features.architecture: Single local SQLite database rebuilt from a JSON seed and the audit log. Items move through a claim/start/complete/verify lifecycle recorded as immutable events. SQL/.schema dumps are the reviewable mirrors.
tasks: Define the schema: features, items, routes, integrations, persona workflows, events, claims
Build setup that rebuilds the database from text sources
Implement the claim/start/complete/verify/release lifecycle
Export reviewable .sql and .schema mirrors on every mutation
testCases: setup rebuilds the database deterministically from seed
claiming an already-claimed item fails without --force
validate fails when a feature has no task or no test case
successCriteria: backlog:validate passes on the seeded database
the database can be deleted and rebuilt in well under a second
useCases: An agent selects the next unblocked, unclaimed item and claims a narrow write scope

personaWorkflows.workflow: Claim and progress a backlog item

features.title: Reusable Codex skills
features.architecture: Two skills, traceability and build-runner, installable into ~/.codex/skills. They encode the audit and lifecycle disciplines so they survive across repos and agents.
tasks: Write the traceability skill that keeps the audit log current
Write the build-runner skill that executes backlog items end to end
Document installation into the agent's skills directory
testCases: Each SKILL.md has valid frontmatter with name and description
successCriteria: The skills reference only generic, portable platform commands
useCases: A new repo adopts the model by installing the two skills and an AGENTS.md

personaWorkflows.workflow: Load the traceability skill before finishing work

features.title: Context broker and workflow profiles
features.architecture: SQLite chooses the context. A workflow profile picks the queries and caps; the broker emits a bounded packet (feature, items, routes, recent audit, next files) in markdown, json, or toon, and forbids bulk-loading generated mirrors.
features.ux: CLI packet output intended to be pasted into, or read by, an agent before it opens any source files.
tasks: Load workflow profiles and merge with defaults
Query SQLite for a bounded packet and derive next files from anchors and routes
Render markdown, json, and toon and write a packet manifest
Audit packet manifests for forbidden bulk files and file-count caps
testCases: context:feature for S07 on the route workflow emits a packet whose nextFiles exclude forbidden bulk files
context:item for S07-TASK-01 emits a toon packet with context, backlogItems, and routes sections
context:audit reports the last manifest as valid
successCriteria: The packet never names a forbidden bulk file
The packet file count never exceeds the profile cap
useCases: An agent asks for the route-workflow packet for a feature and reads only the files it names

personaWorkflows.workflow: Generate a bounded context packet before reading files
personaWorkflows.notes: Stay within nextFiles unless evidence points elsewhere.

features.title: Read-only MCP adapter for ChatGPT Apps review
features.architecture: Future MCP server adapter that exposes ADG read-only governance packets and validation reports with explicit tool annotations, output schemas, and review-ready test cases.
features.ux: ChatGPT Apps reviewers and users can request bounded ADG reports without mutating the governed repo.
tasks: Expose context slice, elicitation graph, UX validation, standards matrix, and deliverable audit as read-only MCP tools
Declare readOnlyHint, openWorldHint, destructiveHint, and outputSchema for every tool
Generate chatgpt-app-submission.json only after tool descriptors match implementation behavior
testCases: Each MCP tool descriptor has explicit safety hints and an outputSchema
Submission test cases cover the main read-only governance workflows
Negative test cases do not trigger ADG tools for unrelated app requests
successCriteria: No MCP tool mutates the governed repo
ChatGPT Apps submission metadata is generated from inspected tool descriptors
useCases: A reviewer asks ChatGPT for the ADG standards matrix and receives a bounded read-only report

integrations.name: ChatGPT Apps MCP
integrations.notes: Deferred until the Codex plugin package is published.

personaWorkflows.workflow: Inspect read-only ADG evidence through MCP tools
personaWorkflows.notes: Tool annotations and output schemas are required before submission.
